campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss the Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority's investment report for March 2026, adopted a new policy about how city property can be used, and addressed staffing challenges including job vacancies and employee retention efforts.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority investment report for March 2026
New policy restricting use of city property for federal civil enforcement activities
City job vacancies and recruitment/retention strategies for employees

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing to address concerns about cleaning up hazardous conditions on certain properties the County identified, and voted to move forward with the cleanup work. The council also adopted a new technology policy and approved a process for residents to request accommodations from the city.
keyTopics: Public hearing on hazardous property cleanup ordered by the County
New City Council Technology Disruption Policy
Process for residents to request reasonable accommodations

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — May 5, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met to approve routine administrative items, including formal approval of the previous meeting's minutes and adoption of resolutions related to managing hazardous vegetation and landscape/lighting maintenance in the city. The council also authorized the Town Manager to enter into several multi-year service agreements with vendors.
keyTopics: Hazardous vegetation declared a public nuisance - brush removal enforcement
Landscape and lighting maintenance resolution approved
Five-year service agreements authorized with multiple vendors
Approval of previous meeting minutes

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 13, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a brief meeting to prepare for upcoming council sessions. They reviewed and finalized the agenda for May 19th, scheduled meetings for the rest of fiscal year 2026-2027, and handled appointments to various city committees and boards.
keyTopics: Finalized May 19, 2026 council agenda
Approved fiscal year 2026-2027 meeting schedule
City Council committee and board appointments
Travel request approval

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: This was a Mountain View City Council meeting on May 14, 2026. The council is reminding residents how to submit questions before meetings and where to find agendas for city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: How to submit public questions before council meetings
Where to find meeting agendas and locations (posted 24+ hours in advance)
Contact info for City Manager's Office (650-903-6301 or CNC@MountainView.gov)
Public documents available at City Clerk's Office, 500 Castro Street

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 5, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-session meeting on May 5, 2026, followed by a regular evening council meeting. The closed session likely covered personnel or legal matters that required confidential discussion, though the specific topics were not disclosed to the public.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting convened (5 P.M.)
Regular council meeting (7 P.M.)
Small business agenda item

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 11, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council held an initial planning session to review Cupertino's proposed budget for fiscal year 2026-27. The meeting also covered the city's rules requiring people who lobby the city to register and report their lobbying activities.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget proposal review and planning
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under city code

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 14, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on May 14, 2026 included a disclosure about legal deadlines for residents. If the city makes certain decisions, residents have 90 days from the decision date to file any lawsuit or legal challenge, or they lose the right to do so.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to sue the city over its decisions
Legal challenges must follow California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6
Residents should act quickly if they want to challenge city decisions in court

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve the city's five-year capital improvement plan for 2027-2031, reviewed the proposed operating budget for fiscal year 2026-27, and adopted a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District. The council also updated its internal procedures handbook and reviewed upcoming meeting schedules.
keyTopics: Approved 2027-2031 capital improvement projects and budget
Reviewed FY 2026-27 operating budget proposal
Adopted Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
Updated City Council procedures and protocols handbook

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on a proposal to build a 6-story affordable housing building with 72 apartments at 450 Lytton Avenue, replacing a city parking lot. The developer is asking for special approval and rule waivers under California's affordable housing law to make the project feasible.
keyTopics: 72 affordable housing units in a 6-story building on 0.43 acres
Project replaces 52-space city-owned surface parking lot (Lot T)
Request for streamlined review and state density bonus concessions
CEQA environmental review underway
